title: Package architecture
text: goldilocks package implements Goldilocks adaptive trial design described Broglio et al. (2014). vignette provides visual overview package functions interconnected.

title: Package architecture
text: diagram shows call graph top-level simulation function (sim_trials()) core engine (survival_adapt()) internal analysis pipeline. Exported functions shown blue. Internal functions shown grey.

title: Package architecture
text: functions fall three layers:

title: Package architecture
text: sim_comp_data(): Generates complete trial dataset calling enrollment(), randomization(), pwe_sim(). survival_adapt(): Simulates single adaptive trial. Generates data via sim_comp_data(), conducts interim analyses using posterior() test_stop_success(), performs final analysis via test_final(). return_trace = TRUE, also retains compact audit trail completed interim look. sim_trials(): Top-level entry point. Runs survival_adapt() across multiple trials (optionally parallel) collates results.

title: Package architecture
text: summarise_sims(): Summarizes output sim_trials(), computing operating characteristics power, expected sample size, stopping probabilities. summarise_trial_trace(): Condenses optional single-trial interim trace one-row stopping-path summary. plot_trial_trace(): Visualizes predictive probabilities, thresholds, enrollment, observed events optional single-trial trace. plot_sim_stopping(): Visualizes marginal, conditional, cumulative, flowchart stopping outcomes enrolled sample sizes across simulated trials. plot_sim_ocs(): Compares success, stopping, expected-sample-size operating characteristics across treatment-effect scenarios. plot_sim_decisions(): Maps simulated predictive probabilities expected-success, continuation, futility regions interim look.

title: Package architecture
text: posterior(): Estimates posterior distribution piecewise exponential hazard rates using conjugate Gamma model. analyse_data(): Applies chosen analysis method (logrank, cox, bayes-surv, bayes-bin, chisq) (imputed) dataset. impute_data(): Imputes missing event times censored subjects using pwe_impute() pwe_sim(). haz_to_prop(): Converts posterior hazard rate draws cumulative incidence proportions via ppwe().

title: Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs
text: Survival many settings – post-surgical mortality, transplant outcomes, oncology early treatment toxicity – shows higher hazard early lower steady-state hazard later. single exponential rate capture regimes; using average rate -states early survival -states late survival. piecewise-exponential model single internal cut-point often good compromise, fitting one hazard early interval another everything afterwards. package handles piecewise hazards two related arguments: cutpoints: positive interior times hazard changes. J−1J - 1 cutpoints JJ intervals therefore JJ hazard rates treatment group. example, cutpoints = 6 produces two intervals, [0,6)[0, 6) [6,∞)[6, \infty). default cutpoints = NULL supplies change times produces single, constant hazard interval. hazard_treatment hazard_control: vectors length JJ giving constant hazard interval. helper prop_to_haz() maps cumulative event probabilities given times corresponding piecewise hazards.

title: Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs
text: Suppose primary endpoint overall survival 24 months. Based prior data, expect control arm : 30% mortality 6 months (“early high-risk” window), 50% mortality 24 months overall. treatment hypothesised attenuate early-period hazard, leaving late-period hazard unchanged. Concretely, target: 18% mortality 6 months treatment arm, 40% mortality 24 months overall. set cut-point 6 months translate proportions piecewise hazards: first column hazard [0,6)[0, 6) months second column hazard 6 months onward. arms higher hazard early window. can sanity-check implied survival probabilities 24 months match specified running cumulative incidence computation back ppwe(): 0.50 0.40 respectively (modulo rounding).

text: Major behavior change goldilocks 0.5.0 earlier. Versions 0.5.0 generated Poisson counts unit-time bins. enrollment() returned rebased integer bin times, sim_comp_data() added independent uniform jitter sorted result. current implementation instead generates continuous arrival times directly exact piecewise-constant Poisson process. Consequently: seeded simulations reproduce enrollment downstream trial results obtained version 0.5.0 earlier; enrollment times operating-characteristic estimates can change, particularly rates low rate change integer time; schedule API now contains internal knots : change lambda_time = 0 lambda_time = NULL constant rate, change lambda_time = c(0, t1, t2) lambda_time = c(t1, t2) piecewise rate. enrollment() treats time zero first patient , matching time origin used throughout goldilocks. first returned enrollment time therefore fixed zero. remaining N_total - 1 arrivals form continuous-time non-homogeneous Poisson process whose rate constant supplied internal knots. Thus, lambda measured enrollments per unit lambda_time; example, time measured months, lambda = 5 means five enrollments per month average. Write internal knots \(0 < \tau_1 < \cdots < \tau_K\), \(\tau_0 = 0\) implicit. enrollment intensity $$   \lambda(t) = \lambda_j, \qquad \tau_{j-1} \le t < \tau_j, $$ \(j = 1, \ldots, K\), \(\lambda(t) = \lambda_{K+1}\) final knot. final rate therefore continues long needed reach N_total; finite accrual horizon function. Arrivals generated exactly time-rescaling theorem. \(E_2, \ldots, E_N\) independent unit-rate exponential variables \(S_i = \sum_{k=2}^E_k\), enrollment times first patient $$T_1 = 0, \qquad T_i = \Lambda^{-1}(S_i),$$ \(\Lambda(t) = \int_0^t \lambda(u)\,du\) cumulative enrollment intensity. construction gives independent Poisson counts disjoint calendar intervals, expected count \(\int_a^b \lambda(u)\,du\) \((,b]\). constant rate, successive enrollment gaps independent Exponential(lambda) variables. rate-change times measured first patient , earlier site-opening trial-activation date. operational delays first patient important, must modelled separately using returned relative times. example, lambda = c(0.3, 0.7, 0.9, 1.2) lambda_time = c(5, 10, 15) specifies average enrollment rates 0.3 \([0,5)\), 0.7 \([5,10)\), 0.9 \([10,15)\), 1.2 time 15 onward. Fractional knots lambda_time = 2.5 handled exactly; unit-time binning post-hoc jitter used.

title: goldilocks 0.5.0.9000
text: Major behavior change goldilocks 0.5.0 earlier: enrollment() now simulates exact continuous-time arrivals piecewise-constant Poisson process inverting cumulative intensity. Fractional enrollment-rate changes handled exactly, runtime depends requested sample size rather number empty unit-time bins. Enrollment schedules now follow internal-knot convention used hazard schedules: lambda_time = NULL represents constant rate, supplied positive internal knot requires one additional lambda value, time zero remains implicit first-patient-origin. sim_comp_data() now consumes continuous enrollment times directly without post-hoc uniform jitter (#55). Previously, enrollment() generated Poisson counts unit-time bins, returned rebased integer bin times, sim_comp_data() added uniform jitter. Existing calls must replace lambda_time = 0 NULL remove leading zero piecewise schedules. Seeded simulations reproduce results version 0.5.0 earlier, operating-characteristic estimates may change, especially fractional knots low enrollment rates. Cox regression now supports imputed_final = TRUE. final log hazard ratios within-imputation variances combined using Rubin’s rules, post_prob_ha reports 1 - P pooled Wald test. Non-imputed Cox analyses unchanged; imputed log-rank chi-square final analyses remain unsupported (#56). Bayesian survival Monte Carlo beta-binomial analyses now use shared N_mcmc argument posterior sampling; separate bin_N argument removed. internal analysis results return posterior mean effect instead posterior draws. survival_adapt() can now return optional, tidy interim decision trace predictive probabilities, thresholds, stopping decisions, arm-level counts, relevant warnings. New helpers summarize plot individual traces simulation stopping outcomes (#57). plot_sim_stopping() now offers marginal, conditional, cumulative, flowchart views stopping outcomes enrolled sample size. Plot subtitles state bar-chart denominators explicitly, flowchart shows trial counts branching futility, continued enrollment, early success successive looks. Bar-chart legends placed beyond plotting region margins sized labels, avoiding overlap across device themes text sizes. Subtitles left-aligned long denominator descriptions stay visible, cumulative view uses compact y-axis label remains clear title. Percentage labels use compact, consistent size across bar-chart views avoid collisions adjacent looks. retained simulation traces supplied, conditional, cumulative, flowchart views include reached looks trial stopped. New plot_sim_ocs() plots success stopping probabilities together expected sample size across treatment-effect simulation scenarios. sim_trials(return_trace = TRUE) now retains compact interim traces across simulations, new plot_sim_decisions() visualizes predictive- probability decision regions interim look. sim_trials() now supports reproducible PSOCK parallel execution Windows explicit backend argument. default Unix fork path retained; backend = "auto" selects appropriate implementation platform. Seeded simulations now also restore caller’s RNG state (#40). Consolidated Bayesian binomial analysis implementation posterior calculations method-specific branches co-located bayes_binomial_test(). Renamed piecewise-exponential Bayesian survival analysis method method = "bayes" method = "bayes-surv" distinguish method = "bayes-bin". Added method = "bayes-bin" Bayesian beta-binomial analysis complete binary outcomes, Monte Carlo, normal approximation, quadrature options treatment-control differences. Cox model analyses now use lower-level survival fit repeated Wald tests, avoiding formula summary overhead simulation hot paths. survival_adapt() now uses h0 null log hazard ratio Cox model tests, allowing non-inferiority testing h0 = log(margin). Added maintainer performance benchmarks simulation hot paths, including posterior probability conversion, posterior sampling, imputation, representative survival_adapt() runs (#42). Harmonized treatment-assignment terminology internal simulation helpers documentation: data use treatment = 1 treatment treatment = 0 control, posterior/imputation array indexing now described separately hazard slices (#35). sim_trials() now accepts seed argument creates independent per-trial "L'Ecuyer-CMRG" random-number streams reproducible simulations, including using multiple cores (#41). ppwe() now computes piecewise-exponential cumulative event probabilities directly cumulative hazard, avoiding row-wise calls PWEALL::pwe() Bayesian posterior summaries (#34). posterior() now computes piecewise-exponential sufficient statistics directly, avoiding survSplit() grouped dplyr summarization simulation hot path. enrollment() randomization() longer grow vectors repeatedly inside simulation loops (#44). sim_trials() summarise_sims() now use dplyr::bind_rows() faster binding simulation result data frames (#43).

title: goldilocks 0.5.0.9000
text: Piecewise Bayesian analyses now retain posterior-draw dimension N_mcmc = 1, avoiding matrix-validation error probability conversion. sim_trials() now uses default alternative hypothesis futility threshold survival_adapt(), omitted arguments define adaptive design (#47). survival_adapt() now requires interim looks strictly increasing, preventing non-chronological duplicated analyses (#48). Piecewise hazard inputs now validated finite non-negative across simulation, imputation, probability helpers. finite maxtime now required final hazard zero (#49). h0 must now single finite value, probability-scale bounds enforced Bayesian analyses (#50). cutpoints API now accepts interior hazard-change times: NULL represents constant hazard, supplied cutpoint requires one additional hazard rate. implicit interval start zero handled internally (#58). Piecewise model inputs require finite, positive, strictly increasing cutpoints finite study endpoint final cutpoint; validation shared simulation probability helpers (#52). enrollment() now validates complete schedule generating data, including integer sample size, finite positive rates, finite strictly increasing knots (#53). Chi-square final analyses now require imputed_final = FALSE, rather averaging chi-square results across multiple imputed final datasets without supported frequentist pooling rule (#51). sim_trials() now defaults serial execution ncores = 1L, matching documentation avoiding unexpected use available cores (#54). Chi-square analyses now error censored subjects followed end_of_study imputed analysis. survival_adapt() now works documented default success futility thresholds interim_look = NULL; thresholds ignored interim looks. survival_adapt(), sim_comp_data(), sim_trials() now validate probability, prior, positive-integer count arguments front, avoiding invalid simulations low-level downstream errors. sim_trials() now falls back ncores = 1L available core count detected. Log-rank Cox analyses now fail clear non-estimable-analysis errors test statistic computed reliably, rather allowing NA NaN values break adaptive decision logic. randomization() now requires two-element positive integer allocation vector, preventing invalid two-arm allocation schedules c(0, 1). prop_to_haz() now rejects invalid cumulative event probabilities, including non-finite values, values outside [0, 1), values decrease time. pwe_impute() now errors maxtime earlier observed conditioning time, preventing imputed records moving backward time. survival_adapt() longer dereferences disabled futility result Fn = 0, futility counters remain inert futility monitoring turned (#33). survival_adapt() sim_trials() now expose empty_interval policy Bayesian piecewise-exponential posterior calculations, allowing empty intervals use legacy propagation, prior-updating, strict errors.
